title: Magnetic Stirrer w/Stir Bar, 3000RPM Magnetic Stir Plate, (No Heating) Stir Plate Max Capacity 3000mL Magnetic Mixer
description:
POWERFUL STIRRING: this magnetic stirrer has an adjustable and powerful stirring speed up to 3000 rpm. You can change and select the suitable stirring speed as per your need.
BIG CAPACITY: This stir plate has the maximum of 3000ML. It is very good choice for home brew yeast starters.
WIDE USE: the ANZESER magnetic stir plate can be used for stirring and mixing in food processing, school laboratory, industry, agriculture and home brew, etc. Not suitable for stirring high viscosity liquids, such as epoxy resins, etc.
EASY USE: Simple single-button design. Stepless adjustable stirring speed, mixing speed regulation up to 3000 rpm, and rotate to the left end is off.
PACKING LIST: Magnetic mixer, power adapter, magnetic stirring bar, instruction manual. If you have any questions, please contact us, we are always at your service.
reviews_5s:
this works great for Lab equipment I have not tried this on volumetric flasks but it might work Now I have a round bottom flask that I use but I think I need different stir bars I would recommend a football shaped stir bar either way it's still works decent and I have used it for a while The only problem I have seen so far is that the lowest setting doesn't even stir If you are trying to reach low speed then put it at a higher one and slowly turn the knob downwards Besides that I haven't seen any problems with it however I would maybe recommend some more bars Because I lost one =( Also something that's funny you can even take off the knob And if you touch the main part then it's actually kind of sticky It's nice that you can take it off for cleaning purposes Also another thing one time my brother poured water all over the stir plate I quickly dried it off and it still worked I still wouldn't recommend pouring water all over it to test it I think it might be waterproof but don't test it stillGood value for the priceConnect and turn the knob to use. Simple function with one easy task. Spin the magnet bar.

I use it for non science stuff but it’s the best way to mix stuff. I use it to mix protein drinks, water beverage mixes and kool-aid…

Don’t use with plastic cups or glasses as it will disfigure and possibly damage the cup/glass. I use actual glass containers for this as the magnet bar will only glide over the glass but no damage to the container.Works greatPlay Video
Not to go Heisenburg but lab stirrers are great in the kitchen. I've used a pair (one heat and stir and one stir only) for many years, my stir only gave out so I replaced it with this one and so far so good.

You can control the stir speed from very slow to really fast, best of all the only moving part is the magnetic stir bead in the bottom, ingredients won't get chopped up as in a blinder. (I've never used one on a super thick soup but for reconstituting beef stock paste into broth they are great.

Anytime I really need to make sure somethign fully disolves I can leave it on a stirrer and know it will be ready to go while I can complete other tasks cooking. The same company makes one that has heat as well and the price on that is crazy low compared to 15-20 years ago when I got mine. If I'm doing a big meal it let's me multi-task better.

If you haven't used one it's worth a try, I can't believe how cheap they are now.

**Safety note - the glassware and stir rods I use for cooking have never been used with chemicals or anything other than food grade materials. Don't reuse lab glassware for safety. That said the gradients on lab glassware are easy to read and you can get a variety of sizes which is great when you are working on receipies that need perfect measure.*I purchased this unit to stir LC in pint bottles. I found it only worked on two settings. It does the job, but finding the sweet spot to get the stir bar to spin is a pain. Would I buy this one again? No.

Update 08-2024
With the right weighted magnets this works very well so upgraded *3 to *5 ... And about whether I would buy or recommend. Yes, get the right accessories and things do work much better.I used this magnetic stirrer to mix my own Hyaluronic acid face serum, and it cut the time to make the serum by more than half. Without the stirrer it takes 8 hours to make the serum. Using the stirrer, it took only 3 hours to make the serum. I plan on using this to make other face serums. Very happy with the purchase!So far so good.Best, easy mixer.I like to make small batches of shellac in varying percentages and this little tool makes that easy as pie. I use a cheap coffee grinder to create shellac powder out of the flakes, pour that powder into whatever volume of denatured alcohol that I need (into a mason jar), drop in a magnetic pellet and turn the centrifuge on. I can fully dissolve my shellac powder into the liquid within about 30 minutes. It is an awesome time saver!
reviews_1s:
I've used this stir plate in 3 minute sessions maybe once or twice a week since I ordered it back in June, and today I went to use it and found that it wouldn't spin. I confirmed that the motor was getting power but just wouldn't start up, so it's definitely a bad motor. I definitely expected more use than the few hours I got out of it.The speed adjustment dial on this ANZESER stirrer doesn't work. There's no way to slow it down. It either works at a fast, whirlpool speed, or not at all. If I try to slow down the speed, it shuts off. I tried to communicate with the seller, but that was useless. They said Sorry it was my fault. How could it be my fault? I tried contacting them again twice, but no reply. It seems like they don't care. Do yourself a favor and buy a different stirrer, because the ANZESER stirrer is worthless, backed by a worthless company and non-existent customer service.Fails to stir even with very thin glass bottomed vessels. Plastic as well.Just opened box. I tested it with tap water. The stirrer bar does not stay centered at the lowest settings.

My bad, it is too late to return. Pure junk!The first time I used this it worked for maybe 5 minutes before stopping and it wouldn't start up again, I was going to return it, but the next day it seemed to work again. The next time I went to mix something (I only got it to mix something every 5-6 months) it started up and then worked again for a couple of minutes. I believe the design or build quality is very poor and the little motor shorts out after a few minutes of use..The product was missing the stir bar so the product is completely useless without it.Turned on the switch, it ran fast for being at lowest setting, and after a few seconds it stopped working and the LED indicator occasionally flickered. A different brand failed after a few months vs. the few seconds I got out of this one.Play Video
I’d like a refund. This product came to me broken. I finally got around to making a yeast starter and decided to test this product beforehand. Sure enough no green light indicating that it’s turned on. I made sure that breaker and fuse was working and plugged in my phone charger to rule out an electrical problem. It’s the product.So it failed during the first test on my kitchen counter right out of the box I took it apart and it looks pretty good. The is an inductor in the speed control so it doesn't waste heat, probably lasts. The failure is misleading. The set screw attaching the internal magnets to the motor rotor comes loose. The motor shaft is thin without a flat. When I spun by hand I felt resistance so I dismissed this possibility. But I noticed it slid up and down on the shaft. It's a special tiny allan as you'd expect.The stirrer worked fine for awhile then stopped spinning the magnets. Company said they would take care of it, but needed photos and purchase info. I gave them both, but they would not replace or respond back even though it was within the warranty period.
